sections.label: Pallavi
sections.ta:
அன்பும் அளவோடெனல் அறியாயோ நாராயணா
நானிலம் நவிலும் உண்மையும் மறந்தாயோ*
sections.translit:
anbum aLavOdenal aRiyAyO nArAyaNA
nAnilam navilum uNmaiyum maRandhAyO
sections.en: Oh Narayana, don’t you know that even love must be within limits? Did you forget this universal truth?

sections.label: Anupallavi
sections.ta:
இமையே அசையாது உமையே நோக்கிடும்
தேவியை விடுத்திப் பாவியை (சற்றே) பாராயோ
sections.translit:
imaiyE asaiyAdhu umaiyE nOkkidum
dhEviyai vidutthip pAviyai (satRE) pArAyO
sections.en: Won’t you just look at this poor soul or sinner for a moment, turning away from Devi, whose eyes are transfixed on you without any movement of her eyelids?

sections.label: Charanam
sections.ta:
க்ஷீரசாகர சாரங்க ப்ரபோ கதிரிடை அலரும்
கமலம் போலுன் ஒளிர்முகமதில் மிளிரும் மந்தஹாசம் படர
புரிந்ததால் செவ்வந்திபோல் அன்னையின் கன்னம் மின்ன
பரிபாஷையில் யுகமாக பரகதி நாடிடும் பராதியைப் பரிதல் எப்போதோ
பண்பும் பாங்கும் அதுவேயென மறந்தாயோ
sections.translit:
kshIrasAgara sAranga prabhO kadhiridai alarum
kamalam pOlun oLirmugamadhil miLirum manthahAsam padara
purindhadhAl sevvandhipOl annaiyin kannam minna
paribAshaiyil yugamAga paragathi nAdidum parAdhiyaip paridhal eppOdhO
*Second line in Pallavi can be rendered as:
paNbum pAngum adhuvEyena maRandhAyO
sections.en: Oh Lord reclining on the milky ocean and wielding the bow, when a smile starts to spread on your lotus-like face and, with the understanding of that expression, Lakshmi’s cheeks shine like red chrysanthemum, several generations pass in this loving exchange. Tell me Lord, when will you have time to look at this poor soul seeking salvation, and show some sympathy? Author’s Note This song is based on Thyagaraja’s description of the mutual love between Lord Vishnu and Goddess Mahalakshmi in one of his songs. Refer to the charanam of “Oh Ranga Sai…”, Kambodhi raga kriti where Thyagaraja complains to Lord Ranganatha: “If you spend all your time in Lakshmi’s company and enjoying exclusively, who will hear my complaints?”. For details of the Saint’s song and translations and interpretations, see: Compositions of Thyagaraja, compiled and edited by T.K. Govinda Rao, Gnanamandir Publications, Indira Nagar, Madras 600020, First Edition May 1995, Page 444. Also see: The Spiritual Heritage of Thyagaraja, C. Ramanujachari, Sri Ramakrishna Math, Mylapore, Madras 600004, Published 1958, Page: Text and Translation of the Songs Section, Page 75.
